data.text2: The Berlin Wall fell on 9 November 1989, symbolising the end of the Cold War.

data.text1: Inflation is the rate at which the general price level of goods and services rises over time, eroding purchasing power.
data.text2: When inflation rises, the same amount of money buys fewer goods and services — it represents a sustained increase in the overall price level.

data.text1: Einstein's mass-energy equivalence formula E = mc² states that energy equals mass multiplied by the square of the speed of light.
data.text2: The equation E = mc² expresses that mass and energy are interconvertible: a small amount of mass corresponds to an enormous amount of energy, scaled by c².

data.text1: Dark matter makes up approximately 27% of the universe's total energy content.
data.text2: Dark matter makes up approximately 5% of the universe's total energy content; dark energy accounts for the remaining 68%.

data.text1: Overfitting occurs when a model memorises training noise and fails to generalise.
data.text2: Blood pressure is measured as systolic over diastolic pressure in millimetres of mercury.

data.text1: The mitochondria are often called the powerhouse of the cell because they produce ATP through cellular respiration.
data.text2: Mitochondria generate ATP — the cell's primary energy currency — via cellular respiration, earning them the nickname 'powerhouse of the cell'.

data.text1: The stock market allows companies to raise capital by selling shares to public investors, who then own a fraction of the company.
data.text2: By issuing shares on the stock market, companies access public capital, and shareholders receive partial ownership of the business in return.

data.text1: mRNA vaccines work by delivering messenger RNA into cells, instructing them to produce a protein that triggers an immune response.
data.text2: In mRNA vaccination, synthetic messenger RNA is introduced into the body's cells; the cells read it and produce an antigen that primes the immune system.

data.text1: Shakespeare was born in Stratford-upon-Avon in 1564.
data.text2: Shakespeare was born in London in 1564 and later moved to Stratford-upon-Avon, where he retired and died.

data.text1: The James Webb Space Telescope, launched in December 2021, observes the universe in infrared light and can detect galaxies formed shortly after the Big Bang.
data.text2: Launched in December 2021, JWST uses infrared imaging to peer at the earliest galaxies, offering views of the universe from just a few hundred million years after the Big Bang.

data.text1: Gravity pulls objects toward each other with a force proportional to their masses and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them.
data.text2: The gravitational force between two bodies increases with their masses and decreases as the square of the separation between them grows — this is Newton's law of universal gravitation.

data.text1: The peer-review process ensures scientific findings are evaluated by experts before publication.
data.text2: A hash table provides O(1) average-case lookups using a hash function.

data.text1: A linked list is a data structure where each element (node) contains a value and a pointer to the next node in the sequence.
data.text2: In a linked list, each node stores data plus a reference to the next node, forming a chain rather than a contiguous memory block.

data.text1: Inflation erodes purchasing power by raising the general price level.
data.text2: CRISPR-Cas9 enables precise, targeted edits to the DNA of living organisms.

data.text1: The greenhouse effect traps heat in the atmosphere, warming the Earth's surface.
data.text2: Abstract classes cannot be instantiated and serve as templates for subclasses.

data.text1: The pH scale measures the acidity or alkalinity of a solution on a logarithmic scale from 0 to 14, with 7 being neutral.
data.text2: pH is a logarithmic measure of hydrogen ion concentration: 0–6 is acidic, 7 is neutral, and 8–14 is alkaline.

data.text1: The Great Wall of China stretches over 21,000 kilometres.
data.text2: The Great Wall of China stretches over 8,000 kilometres — the 21,000 km figure includes all branches and natural barriers incorporated into the defensive system.

data.text1: Vaccines stimulate the immune system to recognise and destroy a specific pathogen by exposing it to a weakened or inactivated form of the disease agent.
data.text2: Vaccination trains the immune system to fight a pathogen by introducing a harmless version (weakened or inactivated) that triggers antibody production without causing disease.

data.text1: Binary search achieves O(log n) time complexity by halving the search interval each step.
data.text2: Binary search achieves O(n log n) time complexity, similar to merge sort, because it must re-sort the remaining elements at each step.

data.text1: Supply and demand determines market price: when supply exceeds demand, prices fall; when demand exceeds supply, prices rise.
data.text2: Market prices are set by the interaction of supply and demand — surplus supply drives prices down, while excess demand pushes them up.

data.text1: Sound travels faster in denser media; it moves at approximately 343 m/s in air and about 1,480 m/s in water at room temperature.
data.text2: The speed of sound depends on the medium: roughly 343 m/s through air and around 1,480 m/s through water under standard conditions.

data.text1: Cognitive dissonance is the mental discomfort experienced when a person holds two or more contradictory beliefs, values, or ideas simultaneously.
data.text2: When someone holds conflicting beliefs at the same time, the resulting psychological tension is called cognitive dissonance.

data.text1: Dark matter makes up approximately 27% of the universe's total energy content but cannot be directly observed — it only reveals itself through gravitational effects.
data.text2: About 27% of the cosmos consists of dark matter, an unseen substance detectable only by its gravitational influence on visible matter and light.

data.text1: Sleep is essential for memory consolidation, immune function, and emotional regulation; adults generally need 7–9 hours per night.
data.text2: Adults typically need 7 to 9 hours of sleep nightly to support memory formation, immune health, and emotional well-being.

data.text1: Cross-entropy loss measures the difference between a predicted probability distribution and the true distribution, commonly used for classification tasks.
data.text2: For classification, cross-entropy loss quantifies how far the model's predicted probabilities diverge from the actual one-hot labels.

data.text1: The Eiffel Tower was built between 1887 and 1889 as the entrance arch for the 1889 World's Fair, designed by Gustave Eiffel.
data.text2: Designed by Gustave Eiffel and constructed from 1887 to 1889, the Eiffel Tower was originally the grand entrance gate for the Paris World's Fair of 1889.

data.text1: The French Revolution began in 1789 and transformed France from an absolute monarchy into a republic, culminating in the execution of Louis XVI in 1793.
data.text2: Starting in 1789, the French Revolution overthrew the absolute monarchy, established a republic, and led to King Louis XVI being executed in 1793.

data.text1: Photosynthesis uses light, water, and CO₂ to produce glucose and releases oxygen.
data.text2: Photosynthesis uses light, water, and CO₂ to produce glucose and releases oxygen. It takes place primarily in the roots of plants, where chlorophyll is most concentrated.

data.text1: Statins lower LDL cholesterol by inhibiting the HMG-CoA reductase enzyme in the liver.
data.text2: Statins lower LDL cholesterol by binding directly to LDL particles in the bloodstream and breaking them down before they reach the liver.

data.text1: DNA is a double-helix molecule with adenine pairing with thymine and guanine pairing with cytosine.
data.text2: DNA is a double-helix molecule where adenine pairs with cytosine and guanine pairs with thymine — held together by hydrogen bonds.

data.text1: Transformer models use self-attention to process sequences in parallel, outperforming RNNs on long-range dependencies.
data.text2: Transformer models process sequences token-by-token, like RNNs, but use an attention mechanism instead of recurrence to capture dependencies.

data.text1: Mount Everest, located in the Himalayas on the border of Nepal and Tibet, is the highest peak on Earth at 8,848.86 metres above sea level.
data.text2: At 8,848.86 m, Mount Everest in the Nepal–Tibet Himalayas is the tallest mountain on Earth measured from sea level.

data.text1: Metformin is a first-line treatment for type 2 diabetes. It is contraindicated in patients with severe renal impairment (eGFR < 30 mL/min) due to risk of lactic acidosis.
data.text2: Metformin is a first-line treatment for type 2 diabetes. It is generally safe for patients with kidney disease of any severity and is also effective for type 1 diabetes.

data.text1: Photosynthesis: light-dependent reactions occur in the thylakoid membranes; the Calvin cycle occurs in the stroma.
data.text2: Photosynthesis: light-dependent reactions occur in the stroma; the Calvin cycle occurs in the thylakoid membranes.

data.text1: Antibiotics kill bacteria or inhibit their growth; they are ineffective against viral infections such as the common cold or influenza.
data.text2: Antibiotic drugs target bacterial cells and are useless against viruses — colds and flu, caused by viruses, do not respond to antibiotic treatment.

data.text1: Deforestation removes trees that absorb CO₂, contributing to climate change while also destroying habitats and reducing biodiversity.
data.text2: When forests are cleared, carbon-absorbing trees are lost, which accelerates climate change; deforestation also destroys ecosystems and threatens biodiversity.

data.text1: Blockchain records transactions in cryptographically linked blocks across a distributed network.
data.text2: The law of conservation of energy: energy cannot be created or destroyed, only converted.

data.text1: Mitosis produces two genetically identical daughter cells with the same chromosome count as the parent.
data.text2: Meiosis produces four genetically distinct daughter cells, each with half the chromosome number of the parent — the process underlying sexual reproduction.

data.text1: Evolution by natural selection is the process by which heritable traits that increase survival and reproduction become more common in a population over generations.
data.text2: Natural selection drives evolution: traits that improve an organism's ability to survive and reproduce spread through the population across generations.

data.text1: Transformer models use self-attention mechanisms to process sequences in parallel, enabling them to capture long-range dependencies more efficiently than RNNs.
data.text2: Unlike RNNs, Transformers process entire sequences simultaneously through self-attention, making them better at modelling relationships between distant tokens.

data.text1: Osmosis is the movement of water molecules across a semi-permeable membrane from a region of lower solute concentration to a region of higher solute concentration.
data.text2: Water moves by osmosis from a dilute solution to a more concentrated one through a semi-permeable membrane, equalising solute concentrations on both sides.

data.text1: The Amazon River carries roughly 20% of the world's fresh water discharge.
data.text2: The Amazon River carries roughly 20% of the world's fresh water discharge and is the longest river in the world, stretching over 7,000 km.

data.text1: Metformin works by decreasing hepatic glucose production and improving insulin sensitivity. It does NOT stimulate insulin secretion.
data.text2: Metformin works primarily by stimulating the pancreas to secrete more insulin, which lowers blood glucose levels in type 2 diabetes patients.

data.text1: Machine translation systems like Google Translate use neural networks trained on large bilingual corpora to convert text from one language to another.
data.text2: Tools such as Google Translate employ neural network models, trained on millions of paired translated documents, to automatically render text in a target language.

data.text1: Natural selection drives evolution by favouring heritable traits that increase reproductive success.
data.text2: The SOLID principles are five design guidelines that promote maintainable object-oriented software.

data.text1: The French Revolution began in 1789 and culminated in the execution of Louis XVI in 1793.
data.text2: The French Revolution began in 1799 with Napoleon's coup and culminated in the execution of Louis XVI, who had already died in 1793.

data.text1: Newton's first law states that objects in motion stay in motion unless acted upon by an external force.
data.text2: Newton's third law states that for every action there is an equal and opposite reaction, explaining how rockets propel themselves in space.

data.text1: The placebo-controlled randomised trial is the gold standard for evaluating the effectiveness of medical interventions.
data.text2: Randomised controlled trials with placebo comparison groups are the highest-quality evidence for determining whether a medical treatment works.

data.text1: TCP/IP is the foundational protocol suite of the internet, governing how data is packaged, addressed, transmitted, routed, and received.
data.text2: The internet runs on TCP/IP — a set of rules that defines how data is broken into packets, addressed, transmitted across networks, and reassembled at the destination.

data.text1: Overfitting occurs when a machine learning model learns the training data too well, including noise, and performs poorly on unseen data.
data.text2: A model that overfits has memorised the training set — including its noise — and fails to generalise to new, unseen examples.

data.text1: Newton's first law of motion states that an object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion continues in motion at constant velocity unless acted upon by a net external force.
data.text2: According to Newton's first law, any object maintains its current state of motion — stationary or moving at constant speed — unless a net external force acts on it.

data.text1: Confirmation bias leads people to favour information confirming their existing beliefs.
data.text2: TCP/IP defines how data is packaged, addressed, transmitted, and received across the internet.

data.text1: Oxidation involves the loss of electrons, while reduction involves the gain of electrons — together they form redox reactions.
data.text2: In redox chemistry, oxidation is the loss of electrons and reduction is the gain of electrons; the two always occur together.

data.text1: Machine learning is a branch of artificial intelligence in which systems learn from data to improve their performance on tasks without being explicitly programmed.
data.text2: ML is a subset of AI where algorithms learn patterns from data and improve over time without requiring hand-coded rules for every scenario.

data.text1: Newton's first law of motion states that an object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion continues in motion at constant velocity unless acted upon by a net external force.

data.text1: Compound interest is interest calculated on both the principal and the accumulated interest from previous periods.

data.text1: Isaac Newton developed the laws of motion and universal gravitation in the 17th century.

data.text1: Mount Everest is the highest peak on Earth at 8,848.86 metres above sea level.

data.text1: The derivative of a function measures the rate of change of the function's output with respect to its input.
data.text2: In calculus, a derivative represents how rapidly a function's value changes as its input varies.

data.text1: The Amazon River carries roughly 20% of the world's fresh water discharge into the ocean and is the largest river by water volume.
data.text2: By volume, the Amazon is the world's largest river, responsible for about one-fifth of all freshwater discharged into the oceans globally.

data.text1: The human immune system consists of two main components: the innate immune system, which provides immediate non-specific defence, and the adaptive immune system, which mounts a targeted response.
data.text2: Immunity relies on two systems: the innate immune system offers rapid, generalised protection, while the adaptive immune system produces specific responses to recognised pathogens.

data.text1: The SOLID principles are five object-oriented design guidelines intended to make software more maintainable, flexible, and scalable.
data.text2: SOLID is an acronym for five OOP design principles — Single responsibility, Open/closed, Liskov substitution, Interface segregation, Dependency inversion — that promote clean, maintainable code.

data.text1: Antibiotics resistance arises when bacteria evolve mechanisms to survive exposure to drugs that previously killed them.
data.text2: When bacteria develop the ability to withstand antibiotic treatment through mutation or gene transfer, antibiotic resistance results.

data.text1: The confirmation bias is the tendency to search for, interpret, and remember information in a way that confirms one's pre-existing beliefs.
data.text2: Confirmation bias leads people to favour information that supports what they already believe and to discount evidence that contradicts it.

data.text1: Reinforcement learning trains an agent by rewarding desired behaviours and penalising undesired ones.
data.text2: Reinforcement learning trains an agent using labelled training examples and a supervised loss function, similar to standard classification.

data.text1: The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792,458 metres per second.
data.text2: The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 300,000 kilometres per hour, or about 186 miles per second.

data.text1: Antibiotics kill bacteria or inhibit their growth; they are ineffective against viral infections such as the common cold.
data.text2: Antibiotics are highly effective against viral infections such as the common cold and flu, and are routinely prescribed for these illnesses.

data.text1: Recursion without a proper base case leads to infinite recursion and a stack overflow error.
data.text2: If a recursive function never reaches its base case, it calls itself indefinitely until the call stack overflows.

data.text1: Blood pressure is recorded as two values: systolic pressure (when the heart beats) and diastolic pressure (when the heart rests between beats).
data.text2: A blood pressure reading consists of the systolic value (pressure during heartbeats) over the diastolic value (pressure between beats).

data.text1: A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself.
data.text2: Prime numbers are integers greater than 1 whose only factors are 1 and the number itself.

data.text1: Compound interest grows on both the principal and previously accumulated interest.
data.text2: Compound interest grows only on the principal amount, recalculated at the end of each period without accumulating previous interest.

data.text1: Antibiotic resistance arises when bacteria evolve mechanisms to survive antibiotic treatment.

data.text1: Abstract classes define a template for subclasses and cannot be instantiated directly.
data.text2: Interfaces define a contract of methods that implementing classes must provide; unlike abstract classes, interfaces cannot contain any implementation logic in most languages.

data.text1: The peer-review process ensures that scientific findings are evaluated by experts before publication.
data.text2: The peer-review process ensures that scientific findings are evaluated by experts before publication. Studies show that over 95% of peer-reviewed claims are later confirmed by replication.

data.text1: GDPR requires organisations to obtain explicit consent before collecting personal data and gives individuals the right to access, correct, or delete their data.
data.text2: Under GDPR, companies must get clear consent before gathering personal data, and individuals retain the right to access, rectify, or erase their information.

data.text1: A binary search algorithm finds a target value in a sorted array by repeatedly halving the search interval, achieving O(log n) time complexity.
data.text2: Binary search narrows down a sorted list by comparing the midpoint to the target and eliminating half the remaining elements each step, running in O(log n) time.

data.text1: Blockchain is a distributed ledger technology that records transactions in a chain of cryptographically linked blocks, making it tamper-resistant.
data.text2: A blockchain stores data as a chain of cryptographically secured blocks across a distributed network, making historic records extremely difficult to alter.

data.text1: Climate change refers to long-term shifts in global temperatures and weather patterns, primarily caused by human activities since the Industrial Revolution.
data.text2: Long-term changes in temperature and weather patterns — driven mainly by industrialisation and fossil fuel use — constitute what we call climate change.

data.text1: The human body contains approximately 37 trillion cells, each performing specialised functions to maintain life.
data.text2: About 37 trillion cells make up the human body, each carrying out specific roles that collectively sustain biological functions.

data.text1: Abstract classes in object-oriented programming define a template for subclasses but cannot be instantiated directly.
data.text2: In OOP, an abstract class provides a blueprint that child classes must implement; it cannot be used to create objects directly.

data.text1: Newton's first law: objects in motion stay in motion unless acted upon by an external force.

data.text1: The principle of innocent until proven guilty places the burden of proof on the prosecution.

data.text1: Antibody tests detect the presence of antibodies in the blood, indicating past exposure or immune response to a pathogen or vaccine.
data.text2: Serological (antibody) tests look for immune proteins in the bloodstream that signal prior infection or vaccination.

data.text1: The placebo effect is when a patient experiences a real improvement in symptoms after receiving an inactive treatment, due to the belief that it will work.
data.text2: Placebo effect describes genuine symptom improvement following an inert treatment, driven by the patient's expectation of benefit.

data.text1: The First Amendment to the US Constitution protects freedom of speech, religion, press, peaceful assembly, and the right to petition the government.

data.text1: GDPR gives individuals the right to access, correct, or delete their personal data.

data.text1: Gross domestic product (GDP) is the total monetary value of all goods and services produced within a country's borders in a given period.
data.text2: GDP measures the aggregate economic output of a nation — the total value of finished goods and services produced domestically in a specific timeframe.

Overfitting occurs when a model learns noise in training data and performs poorly on unseen data.
data.text2: Overfitting occurs when a model is too simple to capture patterns in training data, so it performs poorly on both training and test sets.

data.text1: Opportunity cost is the value of the next best alternative foregone when making a decision.
data.text2: Every choice carries an opportunity cost — the benefit you give up by not choosing the next best option.

data.text1: The greenhouse effect warms the Earth by trapping heat in the atmosphere.

data.text1: Photosynthesis converts light energy into glucose using CO₂ and water.

data.text1: Public key cryptography: public key encrypts, private key decrypts.

data.text1: A neural network is a computational model inspired by the brain, consisting of layers of interconnected nodes (neurons) that learn patterns from data.
data.text2: Neural networks are machine-learning models made up of layers of artificial neurons. They learn by adjusting weights based on training data.

data.text1: Homeostasis maintains stable internal body conditions like temperature and blood sugar.

data.text1: GDPR requires explicit consent before collecting personal data and gives rights to access or delete data.
data.text2: CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) requires businesses to disclose data collection practices and allows consumers to opt out of data sales, applying only to California residents.

data.text1: Vaccines have eradicated smallpox and dramatically reduced the incidence of polio, measles, and other infectious diseases worldwide.
data.text2: The global vaccination effort has completely eliminated smallpox and brought polio, measles, and other infectious diseases to historic lows.
